La p-value du coefficient \(\mu\) du modèle ARMA est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\mu\) à 0.

spec4 = ugarchspec(variance.model=list(model="iGARCH", garchOrder=c(1,1)),
                   mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(1,1)),distribution.model="ghst", fixed.pars=list(mu=0))
fit4 = ugarchfit(spec = spec4, data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit4)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : iGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : ghst 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu        0.0000          NA        NA       NA
## ar1       0.8360    0.040771  20.50474 0.000000
## ma1      -0.8795    0.026394 -33.32166 0.000000
## omega     0.0000    0.000001   0.42705 0.669344
## alpha1    0.0500    0.010725   4.66207 0.000003
## beta1     0.9500          NA        NA       NA
## skew      4.4553    0.237730  18.74094 0.000000
## shape     8.2105    0.207524  39.56424 0.000000
## 
## Robust Standard Errors:
##         Estimate  Std. Error    t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu        0.0000          NA         NA       NA
## ar1       0.8360    0.089646   9.325495 0.000000
## ma1      -0.8795    0.063969 -13.748997 0.000000
## omega     0.0000    0.000007   0.046395 0.962995
## alpha1    0.0500    0.081823   0.611077 0.541148
## beta1     0.9500          NA         NA       NA
## skew      4.4553    1.657675   2.687666 0.007195
## shape     8.2105    1.945453   4.220370 0.000024
## 
## LogLikelihood : 6277.697 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -4.9094
## Bayes        -4.8956
## Shibata      -4.9094
## Hannan-Quinn -4.9044
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                      1.216 0.27008
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]     3.902 0.08496
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]     8.692 0.03159
## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                     0.1907  0.6623
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    2.8696  0.4314
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    3.9098  0.6042
## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests
## ------------------------------------
##             Statistic Shape Scale P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]   0.02043 0.500 2.000  0.8863
## ARCH Lag[5]   1.90918 1.440 1.667  0.4917
## ARCH Lag[7]   2.23658 2.315 1.543  0.6673
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  191.2676
## Individual Statistics:             
## ar1     4.979
## ma1     5.027
## omega  12.918
## alpha1  4.519
## skew   37.624
## shape  54.199
## 
## Asymptotic Critical Values (10% 5% 1%)
## Joint Statistic:          1.49 1.68 2.12
## Individual Statistic:     0.35 0.47 0.75
## 
## Sign Bias Test
## ------------------------------------
##                    t-value   prob sig
## Sign Bias           0.9501 0.3421    
## Negative Sign Bias  0.1463 0.8837    
## Positive Sign Bias  0.4451 0.6563    
## Joint Effect        2.7831 0.4263    
## 
## 
## Adjusted Pearson Goodness-of-Fit Test:
## ------------------------------------
##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20     544.3   2.717e-103
## 2    30     739.9   4.487e-137
## 3    40     843.2   1.069e-151
## 4    50     930.2   4.188e-163
## 
## 
## Elapsed time : 0.07521796

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_1\) du modèle ARMA est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\alpha_1\) à 0. `

spec4 = ugarchspec(variance.model=list(model="iGARCH", garchOrder=c(1,1)),
                   mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(1,1)),distribution.model="ghst", fixed.pars=list(mu=0,alpha1=0))
fit4 = ugarchfit(spec = spec4, data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit4)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : iGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : ghst 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu        0.0000          NA        NA       NA
## ar1       0.8360    0.015768   53.0201  0.00000
## ma1      -0.8795    0.006603 -133.1983  0.00000
## omega     0.0000    0.000000    1.2161  0.22394
## alpha1    0.0000          NA        NA       NA
## beta1     1.0000          NA        NA       NA
## skew     19.7614    0.114260  172.9519  0.00000
## shape     8.2467    0.077424  106.5134  0.00000
## 
## Robust Standard Errors:
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu        0.0000          NA        NA       NA
## ar1       0.8360    1.946653  0.429453  0.66759
## ma1      -0.8795    1.329497 -0.661532  0.50827
## omega     0.0000    0.000056  0.005729  0.99543
## alpha1    0.0000          NA        NA       NA
## beta1     1.0000          NA        NA       NA
## skew     19.7614   63.743469  0.310015  0.75655
## shape     8.2467   17.352464  0.475247  0.63461
## 
## LogLikelihood : 2122.374 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -1.6574
## Bayes        -1.6460
## Shibata      -1.6574
## Hannan-Quinn -1.6533
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                     0.8141 0.36692
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    4.4747 0.01788
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    8.3069 0.04437
## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                      93.61       0
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    342.70       0
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    567.50       0
## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests
## ------------------------------------
##             Statistic Shape Scale P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]     83.73 0.500 2.000       0
## ARCH Lag[5]    249.77 1.440 1.667       0
## ARCH Lag[7]    383.79 2.315 1.543       0
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  83.8809
## Individual Statistics:            
## ar1    3.377
## ma1    3.844
## omega 30.094
## skew  31.005
## shape 49.091
## 
## Asymptotic Critical Values (10% 5% 1%)
## Joint Statistic:          1.28 1.47 1.88
## Individual Statistic:     0.35 0.47 0.75
## 
## Sign Bias Test
## ------------------------------------
##                    t-value      prob sig
## Sign Bias            1.089 2.764e-01    
## Negative Sign Bias   5.234 1.793e-07 ***
## Positive Sign Bias   5.099 3.656e-07 ***
## Joint Effect        56.748 2.909e-12 ***
## 
## 
## Adjusted Pearson Goodness-of-Fit Test:
## ------------------------------------
##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20      1030   1.766e-206
## 2    30      1141   1.441e-221
## 3    40      1240   9.033e-235
## 4    50      1343   4.235e-249
## 
## 
## Elapsed time : 0.06119299

La p-value du coefficient \(ar_1\) du modèle ARMA est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(ar_1\) à 0.

ARCH-M

Gaussienne inverse asymétrique

On estime un modèle ARMA(1,1)-ARCH-M(1,1) avec une distribution nig. Il n’est donc pas nécessaire de prendre en compte la saisonnalité car dans le projet 1 nous avons vu qu’il n’y en avait pas.

spec5 = ugarchspec(mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(1,1),archm=TRUE),distribution.model="nig")
fit5 = ugarchfit(spec = spec5,data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit5)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : nig 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu     -0.000645    0.000875  -0.73733 0.460924
## ar1     0.851876    0.083822  10.16297 0.000000
## ma1    -0.889839    0.073498 -12.10705 0.000000
## archm   0.080845    0.056425   1.43279 0.151918
## omega   0.000003    0.000002   2.04074 0.041277
## alpha1  0.059875    0.010250   5.84148 0.000000
## beta1   0.930683    0.013328  69.83147 0.000000
## skew   -0.093555    0.040769  -2.29479 0.021745
## shape   1.951076    0.358647   5.44011 0.000000
## 
## Robust Standard Errors:
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu     -0.000645    0.001144  -0.56360 0.573025
## ar1     0.851876    0.093654   9.09599 0.000000
## ma1    -0.889839    0.081903 -10.86458 0.000000
## archm   0.080845    0.067950   1.18976 0.234140
## omega   0.000003    0.000003   0.98527 0.324493
## alpha1  0.059875    0.028266   2.11830 0.034149
## beta1   0.930683    0.032107  28.98668 0.000000
## skew   -0.093555    0.041144  -2.27384 0.022975
## shape   1.951076    0.411543   4.74088 0.000002
## 
## LogLikelihood : 6895.469 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -5.3906
## Bayes        -5.3700
## Shibata      -5.3906
## Hannan-Quinn -5.3831
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                     0.6576 0.41743
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    3.0770 0.42432
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    8.0298 0.05625
## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                    0.01581  0.8999
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]   1.39808  0.7649
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]   2.13399  0.8884
## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests
## ------------------------------------
##             Statistic Shape Scale P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]   0.02182 0.500 2.000  0.8826
## ARCH Lag[5]   1.21105 1.440 1.667  0.6715
## ARCH Lag[7]   1.50646 2.315 1.543  0.8201
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  17.9654
## Individual Statistics:             
## mu     0.2030
## ar1    0.2861
## ma1    0.2703
## archm  0.1932
## omega  1.2774
## alpha1 0.9695
## beta1  0.8950
## skew   0.7026
## shape  1.2199
## 
## Asymptotic Critical Values (10% 5% 1%)
## Joint Statistic:          2.1 2.32 2.82
## Individual Statistic:     0.35 0.47 0.75
## 
## Sign Bias Test
## ------------------------------------
##                    t-value   prob sig
## Sign Bias          1.00379 0.3156    
## Negative Sign Bias 0.04617 0.9632    
## Positive Sign Bias 0.13032 0.8963    
## Joint Effect       2.34397 0.5042    
## 
## 
## Adjusted Pearson Goodness-of-Fit Test:
## ------------------------------------
##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20     44.46    0.0008153
## 2    30     49.43    0.0103845
## 3    40     65.38    0.0051084
## 4    50     76.29    0.0075375
## 
## 
## Elapsed time : 1.440751

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_0\) est supérieure à 5%, cependant sa valeur (0.000003 ) est strictement positive donc il reste inchangé.

La p-value du coefficient \(\mu\) du modèle ARMA est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\mu\) à 0.

spec5 = ugarchspec(mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(1,1),archm=TRUE),distribution.model="nig",fixed.pars=list(mu=0))
fit5 = ugarchfit(spec = spec5,data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit5)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : nig 
##

##         Estimate  Std. Error  t value Pr(>|t|)

## 
## Information Criteria

## Bayes        -5.3729

## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    2.9404 0.50971

## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals

## Lag[1]                   0.002954  0.9567
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]  1.282093  0.7932

## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests

##             Statistic Shape Scale P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]   0.03155 0.500 2.000  0.8590
## ARCH Lag[5]   1.11576 1.440 1.667  0.6989
## ARCH Lag[7]   1.42485 2.315 1.543  0.8363
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  15.8696
## Individual Statistics:             
## ar1    0.2811
## ma1    0.2629
## archm  0.1211

## alpha1 1.0268
## beta1  0.9640

## shape  1.1992
##

## Joint Statistic:          1.89 2.11 2.59
## Individual Statistic:     0.35 0.47 0.75
## 
## Sign Bias Test

##                    t-value   prob sig
## Sign Bias           1.1264 0.2601    
## Negative Sign Bias  0.1625 0.8709    
## Positive Sign Bias  0.1296 0.8969    
## Joint Effect        2.6532 0.4482    
## 
## 
## Adjusted Pearson Goodness-of-Fit Test:
## ------------------------------------
##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20     34.94      0.01420

## 4    50     60.44      0.12663
##

## Elapsed time : 1.332617

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_0\) est supérieure à 5%, cependant sa valeur est strictement positive donc il reste inchangé.

La p-value du coefficient \(archm\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(archm\) à 0.

Hyperbolique généralisée skew t

On estime un modèle ARMA(1,1)-ARCH-M(1,1) avec une distribution ghst. Il n’est donc pas nécessaire de prendre en compte la saisonnalité car dans le projet 1 nous avons vu qu’il n’y en avait pas.

spec5 = ugarchspec(mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(1,1),archm=TRUE),distribution.model="ghst")
fit5 = ugarchfit(spec = spec5,data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit5)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : ghst 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu     -0.000599    0.000878  -0.68259 0.494865
## ar1     0.859797    0.072750  11.81854 0.000000

## beta1   0.932717    0.027757  33.60262 0.000000

## 
## LogLikelihood : 6892.76 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -5.3885
## Bayes        -5.3679
## Shibata      -5.3885
## Hannan-Quinn -5.3810
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                     0.5372 0.46358
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    2.9699 0.49095
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    7.9390 0.06071
## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals

## Lag[1]                     0.0285  0.8659
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    1.4871  0.7431
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    2.2409  0.8744
## d.o.f=2
##

##             Statistic Shape Scale P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]   0.01253 0.500 2.000  0.9109
## ARCH Lag[5]   1.26976 1.440 1.667  0.6549
## ARCH Lag[7]   1.55907 2.315 1.543  0.8094
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  22.3647
## Individual Statistics:             
## mu     0.2018

## ma1    0.2736

## beta1  0.9782

## shape  1.9941
##

## Negative Sign Bias 0.04176 0.9667

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_0\) est supérieure à 5%, cependant sa valeur est strictement positive donc il reste inchangé.

La p-value du coefficient \(\mu\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\mu\) à 0.

spec5 = ugarchspec(mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(1,1),archm=TRUE),distribution.model="ghst",fixed.pars=list(mu=0))
fit5 = ugarchfit(spec = spec5,data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit5)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : ghst 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error  t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu      0.000000          NA       NA       NA
## ar1     0.874974    0.393799  2.22188 0.026291
## ma1    -0.909846    0.348654 -2.60960 0.009065
## archm   0.042235    0.061052  0.69178 0.489074
## omega   0.000003    0.000002  1.92287 0.054497
## alpha1  0.059791    0.010803  5.53464 0.000000
## beta1   0.930417    0.013782 67.50802 0.000000
## skew   -0.368594    0.151344 -2.43548 0.014872
## shape   8.171164    1.390660  5.87575 0.000000
## 
## Robust Standard Errors:
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu      0.000000          NA        NA       NA
## ar1     0.874974    3.582349  0.244246 0.807040
## ma1    -0.909846    3.168750 -0.287131 0.774012
## archm   0.042235    0.561678  0.075194 0.940060
## omega   0.000003    0.000004  0.776794 0.437280
## alpha1  0.059791    0.035472  1.685575 0.091878
## beta1   0.930417    0.043240 21.517655 0.000000
## skew   -0.368594    1.292934 -0.285084 0.775580
## shape   8.171164    4.341168  1.882250 0.059802
## 
## LogLikelihood : 6892.052 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -5.3887
## Bayes        -5.3704
## Shibata      -5.3887
## Hannan-Quinn -5.3821
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                     0.4637 0.49590
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    2.8627 0.55958
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    7.8783 0.06387
## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                   0.008204  0.9278
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]  1.343291  0.7783
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]  2.058324  0.8979
## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests
## ------------------------------------
##             Statistic Shape Scale P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]   0.02233 0.500 2.000  0.8812
## ARCH Lag[5]   1.16197 1.440 1.667  0.6855
## ARCH Lag[7]   1.46221 2.315 1.543  0.8289
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  19.439
## Individual Statistics:             
## ar1    0.2791
## ma1    0.2553
## archm  0.1108
## omega  1.3636
## alpha1 1.1345
## beta1  1.0459
## skew   0.7971
## shape  1.2191
## 
## Asymptotic Critical Values (10% 5% 1%)
## Joint Statistic:          1.89 2.11 2.59
## Individual Statistic:     0.35 0.47 0.75
## 
## Sign Bias Test
## ------------------------------------
##                    t-value   prob sig
## Sign Bias          1.14970 0.2504    
## Negative Sign Bias 0.15823 0.8743    
## Positive Sign Bias 0.09393 0.9252    
## Joint Effect       2.67659 0.4442    
## 
## 
## Adjusted Pearson Goodness-of-Fit Test:
## ------------------------------------
##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20     39.23     0.004123
## 2    30     49.97     0.009089
## 3    40     56.87     0.032148
## 4    50     75.55     0.008793
## 
## 
## Elapsed time : 1.520444

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_1\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\alpha_1\) à 0.

spec5 = ugarchspec(mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(1,1),archm=TRUE),distribution.model="ghst",fixed.pars=list(mu=0, alpha1=0))
fit5 = ugarchfit(spec = spec5,data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit5)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : ghst 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error     t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu      0.000000          NA          NA       NA
## ar1     0.821081    0.113107     7.25933 0.000000
## ma1    -0.861118    0.102079    -8.43580 0.000000
## archm   0.034185    0.015118     2.26125 0.023744
## omega   0.000000    0.000000     1.89603 0.057957
## alpha1  0.000000          NA          NA       NA
## beta1   0.999000    0.000073 13710.90319 0.000000
## skew   -0.127425    0.134423    -0.94794 0.343161
## shape   8.199632    0.968921     8.46265 0.000000
## 
## Robust Standard Errors:
##         Estimate  Std. Error    t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu      0.000000          NA         NA       NA
## ar1     0.821081    0.273305    3.00427 0.002662
## ma1    -0.861118    0.253583   -3.39581 0.000684
## archm   0.034185    0.027047    1.26389 0.206271
## omega   0.000000    0.000002    0.14063 0.888159
## alpha1  0.000000          NA         NA       NA
## beta1   0.999000    0.000885 1129.11938 0.000000
## skew   -0.127425    0.426120   -0.29904 0.764913
## shape   8.199632   19.322632    0.42435 0.671308
## 
## LogLikelihood : 6768.653 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -5.2929
## Bayes        -5.2769
## Shibata      -5.2929
## Hannan-Quinn -5.2871
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                     0.6876  0.4070
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    3.6752  0.1433
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    8.5548  0.0357
## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals

## Lag[1]                      39.88 2.706e-10
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    126.87 0.000e+00
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    200.68 0.000e+00
## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests
## ------------------------------------
##             Statistic Shape Scale   P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]     26.84 0.500 2.000 2.209e-07
## ARCH Lag[5]     90.26 1.440 1.667 0.000e+00
## ARCH Lag[7]    130.15 2.315 1.543 0.000e+00
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  714.7149
## Individual Statistics:              
## ar1    0.29089
## ma1    0.26893
## archm  0.08075
## omega 83.93334
## beta1  7.82708
## skew   0.16648
## shape  1.71692
##

##

##                    t-value      prob sig
## Sign Bias            1.076 2.820e-01    
## Negative Sign Bias   4.424 1.010e-05 ***
## Positive Sign Bias   4.109 4.092e-05 ***
## Joint Effect        39.754 1.201e-08 ***
## 
## 
## Adjusted Pearson Goodness-of-Fit Test:
## ------------------------------------
##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20     73.81    2.112e-08
## 2    30    107.74    5.440e-11
## 3    40    119.46    4.297e-10
## 4    50    111.24    9.690e-07
## 
## 
## Elapsed time : 0.9901869

La p-value du coefficient \(archm\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(archm\) à 0.

GARCH

Gaussienne inverse asymétrique

On estime un modèle ARMA(1,1)-GARCH(1,1) avec une distribution nig. Il n’est donc pas nécessaire de prendre en compte la saisonnalité car dans le projet 1 nous avons vu qu’il n’y en avait pas.

spec6 = ugarchspec(distribution.model="nig")
fit6 = ugarchfit(spec = spec6, data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit6)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : nig 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error  t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu      0.000586    0.000103   5.6790 0.000000
## ar1     0.879795    0.040665  21.6350 0.000000
## ma1    -0.914095    0.035556 -25.7086 0.000000
## omega   0.000003    0.000002   1.7274 0.084097
## alpha1  0.062584    0.012323   5.0785 0.000000
## beta1   0.927815    0.015907  58.3292 0.000000
## skew   -0.097332    0.038887  -2.5029 0.012317
## shape   1.953719    0.356761   5.4763 0.000000
## 
## Robust Standard Errors:
##         Estimate  Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu      0.000586    0.000141   4.15903 0.000032
## ar1     0.879795    0.056231  15.64620 0.000000
## ma1    -0.914095    0.046734 -19.55961 0.000000
## omega   0.000003    0.000005   0.74351 0.457170
## alpha1  0.062584    0.037756   1.65760 0.097398
## beta1   0.927815    0.043117  21.51847 0.000000
## skew   -0.097332    0.038982  -2.49685 0.012530
## shape   1.953719    0.419705   4.65498 0.000003
## 
## LogLikelihood : 6894.525 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -5.3906
## Bayes        -5.3723
## Shibata      -5.3906
## Hannan-Quinn -5.3840
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                     0.4617  0.4968
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5]    2.8413  0.5733
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9]    7.8758  0.0640
## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Squared Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value
## Lag[1]                  6.378e-06  0.9980
## Lag[2*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][5] 1.225e+00  0.8069
## Lag[4*(p+q)+(p+q)-1][9] 1.910e+00  0.9155
## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests
## ------------------------------------
##             Statistic Shape Scale P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]   0.04174 0.500 2.000  0.8381
## ARCH Lag[5]   1.08108 1.440 1.667  0.7090
## ARCH Lag[7]   1.39797 2.315 1.543  0.8416
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  14.0493
## Individual Statistics:              
## mu     0.09913
## ar1    0.27819
## ma1    0.25840
## omega  0.98337
## alpha1 1.07977
## beta1  1.02797
## skew   0.59865
## shape  1.17879
## 
## Asymptotic Critical Values (10% 5% 1%)
## Joint Statistic:          1.89 2.11 2.59
## Individual Statistic:     0.35 0.47 0.75
## 
## Sign Bias Test
## ------------------------------------
##                    t-value   prob sig
## Sign Bias           1.2701 0.2042    
## Negative Sign Bias  0.2635 0.7922    
## Positive Sign Bias  0.1068 0.9150    
## Joint Effect        3.0792 0.3796    
## 
## 
## Adjusted Pearson Goodness-of-Fit Test:
## ------------------------------------
##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20     26.66      0.11284
## 2    30     40.35      0.07843
## 3    40     42.84      0.30987
## 4    50     55.16      0.25323
## 
## 
## Elapsed time : 0.989341

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_0\) est supérieure à 5%, cependant sa valeur est strictement positive donc il reste inchangé.

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_1\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\alpha_1\) à 0

spec6 = ugarchspec(distribution.model="nig",fixed.pars=list(alpha1=0))
fit6 = ugarchfit(spec = spec6, data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit6)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : nig

## 
## LogLikelihood : 6781.853 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##

## d.o.f=2

## d.o.f=2
## 
## Weighted ARCH LM Tests
## ------------------------------------
##             Statistic Shape Scale   P-Value
## ARCH Lag[3]     28.96 0.500 2.000 7.395e-08
## ARCH Lag[5]     96.28 1.440 1.667 0.000e+00
## ARCH Lag[7]    139.20 2.315 1.543 0.000e+00
## 
## Nyblom stability test
## ------------------------------------
## Joint Statistic:  734.2421
## Individual Statistics:              
## mu     0.08872
## ar1    0.26565
## ma1    0.25708

## beta1  7.71426
## skew   0.51741

## 4    50     88.46    4.722e-04
## 
## 
## Elapsed time : 0.825877

La p-value du coefficient \(\mu\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\mu\) à 0.

Hyperbolique généralisée skew t

On estime un modèle ARMA(1,1)-GARCH(1,1) avec une distribution ghst. Il n’est donc pas nécessaire de prendre en compte la saisonnalité car dans le projet 1 nous avons vu qu’il n’y en avait pas.

spec6 = ugarchspec(distribution.model="ghst")
fit6 = ugarchfit(spec = spec6, data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit6)
## 
## *---------------------------------*
## *          GARCH Model Fit        *
## *---------------------------------*
## 
## Conditional Variance Dynamics    
## -----------------------------------
## GARCH Model  : sGARCH(1,1)
## Mean Model   : ARFIMA(1,0,1)
## Distribution : ghst 
## 
## Optimal Parameters
## ------------------------------------
##         Estimate  Std. Error     t value Pr(>|t|)
## mu      0.000402    0.000031     13.1648 0.000000
## ar1     0.991077    0.002510    394.9243 0.000000
## ma1    -1.000000    0.000030 -33252.7453 0.000000

## 
## Nyblom stability test

## Joint Statistic:  11.2997

## mu     0.12354
## ar1    0.03071

## Elapsed time : 1.549689

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_0\) est supérieure à 5%, cependant sa valeur est strictement positive donc il reste inchangé.

La p-value du coefficient \(\alpha_1\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\alpha_1\) à 0

spec6 = ugarchspec(distribution.model="ghst", fixed.pars=list(alpha1=0))
fit6 = ugarchfit(spec = spec6, data = rt,out.sample=length(rtt),solver="hybrid")
show(fit6)
## 
## *---------------------------------*

## *---------------------------------*
##

## skew   -0.253787    1.495187   -0.16974 0.865218
## shape   8.199267   18.600767    0.44080 0.659356
## 
## LogLikelihood : 6768.698 
## 
## Information Criteria
## ------------------------------------
##                     
## Akaike       -5.2929
## Bayes        -5.2769
## Shibata      -5.2929
## Hannan-Quinn -5.2871
## 
## Weighted Ljung-Box Test on Standardized Residuals
## ------------------------------------
##                         statistic p-value

## d.o.f=2
## H0 : No serial correlation

##   group statistic p-value(g-1)
## 1    20     73.19    2.694e-08

La p-value du coefficient \(\mu\) est la pvalue la plus élevée (sans considérer \(\alpha_0\), skew et shape) et elle est supérieure à 5%. On rejette \(H_0\) du test de significativité des coefficients et fixe la valeur de \(\mu\) à 0
